title: NYT Crossword Puzzle
description: A single NYT crossword puzzle as stored in this archive.

acrossmap.description: Unused; always null in this archive.

admin.description: Administrative flag; always false or null.

autowrap.description: Unused; always null in this archive.

bbars.description: Bottom-bar positions (encoded cell indices). Null when absent.

circles.description: Per-cell circle indicators (parallel to grid). Non-zero marks a circled cell. Null when absent.

clues.description: Clue text, grouped by direction. Each string is formatted as "<number>. <clue text>".

code.description: Unused; always null in this archive.

copyright.description: Copyright notice, e.g. "2004, The New York Times".

date.description: Publication date in M/D/YYYY format (no zero-padding).

downmap.description: Unused; always null in this archive.

grid.description: Flat row-major grid. Length equals size.cols × size.rows. Each element is one of: a single uppercase letter (normal cell), "." (black square), " " (blank/unfilled cell), or a multi-character string for a rebus cell (e.g. "DUCK", "AND", "&").

gridnums.description: Clue-start numbers, parallel to grid. Positive integer where a numbered clue starts; 0 otherwise. Length equals size.cols × size.rows.

hastitle.description: Whether the puzzle has a standalone title (present in newer files only).

hold.description: Unused; always null in this archive.

id.description: Internal puzzle identifier. Null in most entries.

id2.description: Secondary internal identifier. Null in most entries.

interpretcolors.description: When non-null, the string "true" signals that circle shading should be interpreted as colors.

jnotes.description: Constructor or judge notes, if any.

key.description: Unused; always null in this archive.

mini.description: True if this is a mini puzzle. Null/false for standard puzzles.

notepad.description: Notepad text shown to solvers, if any.

rbars.description: Right-bar positions (encoded cell indices). Null when absent.

shadecircles.description: When non-null, the string "true" indicates circles should be rendered as shaded squares.

size.description: Grid dimensions. Most puzzles are 15×15 (weekday) or 21×21 (Sunday).

title.description: Formatted puzzle title, e.g. "NY TIMES, FRI, FEB 20, 2004".

track.description: Unused; always null in this archive.

type.description: Puzzle type code. Null in this archive.

uniclue.description: Whether the puzzle uses a single unified clue list (present in newer files only).

valid.description: Validity flag (present in a small number of files).
